 {"id":"bd-3hoe","title":"FTS maintenance: verify/rebuild command","description":"## What
Provide a CLI command to verify and rebuild FTS indexes with progress.

## Why
Users need a safe recovery path when search results are inconsistent.

## How
- `wa search fts verify` checks row counts and index integrity
- `wa search fts rebuild` runs a rebuild with progress output

## Success Criteria
- Commands are safe and idempotent
- Progress is visible and redacted","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.401466312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.408157242Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hoe","depends_on_id":"bd-2i2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.408113921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Scheduler interval logic + rate limiting
- Alert payload redaction and counts

## Success Criteria

+{"id":"bd-3iax","title":"IPC config + socket lifecycle design","description":"## What
Define IPC config options and socket lifecycle management.

## Why
We need safe defaults and predictable file paths for local clients.

## How
- Config: enabled flag, socket path, permissions
- Lifecycle: create/remove socket, handle stale sockets

## Success Criteria
- Socket path is deterministic and safe
- Startup/teardown handles crashes cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBear","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:24.280472849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:08:46.985540899Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T06:08:46.985462523Z","close_reason":"Added IPC config/path/permissions + layout resolution; set socket permissions; updated hot-reload rules and tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iax","depends_on_id":"bd-15ds","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:24.287170263Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-3iax","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Summary
- Added `ipc` config with `enabled`, `socket_path`, and `permissions` (default 0o600).
- Workspace layout now resolves IPC socket from config (absolute or relative to `.wa/`).
- Path normalization expands `~` for `ipc.socket_path`.
- Permission warnings now honor `ipc.permissions` and skip when disabled.
- IPC bind sets socket file permissions; stale sockets still removed on bind.
- Hot-reload marks IPC changes as restart-required.

Tests
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
","created_at":"2026-02-04T06:08:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kzu","title":"TUI/Web surfaces for saved searches","description":"## What
Expose saved searches in TUI and web dashboards.

## Why
Operators should be able to view/trigger saved searches without memorizing CLI flags.

## How
- TUI: list saved searches, run on demand, toggle enabled
- Web: read-only list + last run status (no mutations yet)

## Risks
- Feature gating: keep behind tui/web feature flags

## Success Criteria
- Saved searches visible in TUI and web
- No direct DB reads from UI; use shared query client","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:54.854781244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:37.424847534Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:54.864273971Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-37f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:30.257461340Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-4enj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:33.800434310Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:37.424812299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:35.220851055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Define config fields for per-pane priorities and capture budgets.

- Exercise completions and aliases in a scripted shell session with verbose logs enabled.
- Verify help output and verbosity tiers are stable (default/verbose/debug).

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.20","title":"E2E suite completeness: runner --all covers all cases + stable case registry","description":"# Task: E2E suite completeness (case registry)

## Goal
Make sure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` is a comprehensive, maintained suite that:
- runs every **registered** E2E scenario (not just the ones under `wa-4vx.10.*`)
- produces stable artifacts per case
- prints a clear PASS/FAIL summary

This task exists to avoid coupling “runner quality” to “suite completeness”.

## Requirements
- Maintain a single authoritative registry of cases (names + descriptions).
- The registry must also record (at least):
  - prerequisites (e.g., requires WezTerm, requires docker, requires feature flags)
  - whether the case is part of the default `--all` run
  - a human-readable “why this case exists” note (what it protects)

- `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` iterates that registry.
- Each case has:
  - deterministic steps
  - explicit assertions
  - artifacts directory
  - clear failure summary

## Cross-repo case coverage (important)
Some E2E scenarios live outside `wa-4vx.10.*` (for example setup safety, new IPC lanes, and UX overlays):
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10` (wa setup idempotency)
- `wa-4vx.2.7.3` (status_update IPC lane)
- `wa-nu4.1.6.4` (pane reservations)
- `wa-0go.7` (natural-language event descriptions)
- `wa-5em.9` (rollback visualization tests/E2E)
- `wa-dug.7` (environment detection + recommendations)
- `wa-tp4.8` (suggestion engine)
- `wa-nu4.4.3.5` (distributed E2E tests; non-default unless distributed feature enabled)
- `wa-9lh` (quick-fix suggestions E2E)
- `wa-am5` (dry-run mode E2E)
- `wa-jl5` (notification webhook delivery E2E)
- `wa-ugg` (timeline correlation E2E)
- `wa-p3i` (watch-and-notify mode E2E)

Suite completeness means the registry accounts for these cases too.
Implementation options (either is acceptable; pick the simplest):
- unify everything under `./scripts/e2e_test.sh` as runnable `--case` entries, OR
- allow registry entries to delegate to another script (adapter entry) while still producing the same artifacts contract.

## Change discipline
- Adding a new E2E case requires updating the registry.
- CI should catch:
  - “case exists but not in registry”
  - “case is in registry but not included in default suite” (unless explicitly tagged as non-default with justification)

## Registry
- This bead *is* the registry. Update the case list here when new E2E cases are added.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A developer can run `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` and trust it covers the full default suite.
- The suite remains usable even as cases evolve across phases.
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10`, `wa-4vx.2.7.3`, `wa-nu4.1.6.4`, `wa-0go.7`, `wa-5em.9`, `wa-dug.7`, and `wa-tp4.8` are represented in the case registry (directly or via adapter), with stable artifacts.


## Testing
- Registry correctness tests:
  - Add a check that enumerates available E2E cases and fails if any are missing from the registry.
  - Add a check that fails if the registry references a non-existent case.

- `--all` behavior tests:
  - Include a small dummy registry in test mode to verify:
    - ordering and selection flags
    - summary format
    - per-case artifact dir naming is stable

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.21","title":"E2E script: wa stop shuts down watcher (lock released, restart works)","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa stop

## Goal
Validate the user-facing shutdown path:
- wa watch is running
- wa stop stops it gracefully
- the lock is released
- restart works

This is a key UX reliability story for real users.

## Scenario
- Start mux server and spawn a dummy pane that prints a unique token.
- Start wa watch in workspace A.
- Wait until the token is persisted (wa robot search).
- Run wa stop --workspace A.

Assertions
- wa stop returns success.
- wa watch process exits.
- workspace lock is released.
- Starting wa watch again in workspace A succeeds.

## Artifacts
- wa watch logs
- wa stop logs/output
- effective config

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- Failures clearly indicate whether the bug is in:
  - lock metadata
  - signal handling
  - shutdown flushing


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add a negative case: `wa stop` when no watcher is running must return a clear, non-destructive message (and still exit 0/1 consistently as defined).
  - Assert that lock release is proven by attempting a second start (not by checking files alone).

- Artifact validation:
  - Include watcher PID/lock metadata evidence in artifacts so failures are actionable.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.22","title":"E2E script: wa doctor (healthy/broken) with verbose logs + artifacts","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa doctor

## Goal
Prove `wa doctor` works end-to-end as a **deterministic** health-check surface with **excellent logs/artifacts**.

Unit tests can validate individual checks, but this E2E case ensures the full command (arg parsing → config resolution → checks → output) behaves correctly and remains user-friendly.

## Scenario(s)

### Scenario A: Healthy workspace
- Create a fresh temp workspace (isolated DB/log dirs).
- Ensure stubbed dependencies report “healthy”:
  - wezterm CLI available + returns a plausible `wezterm cli list` output
  - DB opens and schema version matches
- Run:
  - `wa doctor --format json`

### Scenario B: Broken workspace (actionable failures)
Run doctor against controlled failures, one at a time:
- Missing wezterm CLI.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB cannot be opened / schema mismatch.

## Assertions
- Exit codes are correct and stable:
  - healthy → `0`
  - broken → non-zero
- JSON output is machine-parseable and contains:
  - a concise overall status
  - per-check status + actionable remediation hints
- No secrets appear in:
  - stdout JSON
  - stderr logs
  - artifact files

## Artifacts / logging contract
- Capture and store per-scenario artifacts in the standard E2E harness layout:
  - stdout (raw)
  - stderr (raw)
  - resolved workspace paths
  - any generated diagnostic files
  - timing summary (start/end + durations per step)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script is deterministic and can run in CI.
- When it fails, the artifact bundle is sufficient to debug quickly.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure each “broken workspace” scenario fails for the intended reason (not cascading unrelated failures).
  - Add assertions that the remediation hints include stable `code` values so automation can key off them.

- Artifact validation:
  - Ensure the script stores both the raw doctor output and a normalized “check summary” extraction for quick debugging.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:18:28.342228068Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:59:43.823781370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:59:43.823640749Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-e2e","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-4vx.10.23","title":"E2E script: accounts refresh (fake caut) + pick preview + redaction","description":"# Task: E2E test script — accounts refresh + pick preview (fake caut)

## Goal
Validate the accounts/usage integration end-to-end without network access:
- `wa robot accounts refresh` invokes `caut` via wrapper, updates DB mirror
- `wa robot accounts` shows deterministic account state + recommended pick
- logs/artifacts remain secret-safe and actionable

This protects a high-risk integration surface (external CLI drift + error handling).

## Scenario
Run in an isolated temp workspace with a stubbed `caut` executable on `PATH`.

### Setup
- Create a temp workspace dir (isolated DB/log paths).
- Create a temp `bin/` dir with a **fake caut** script that:
  - supports `caut refresh --service openai --format json`
  - supports `caut usage --service openai --format json`
  - returns fixture JSON for a few accounts
  - can be toggled to return:
    - non-zero exit + stderr message
    - invalid JSON

### Steps
1. Run: `wa robot accounts refresh --service openai`
   - Assert: exit 0, output schema is valid, DB mirror updated.
2. Run: `wa robot accounts --service openai`
   - Assert: deterministic ordering, `recommended` matches policy.
3. Negative path:
   - Make fake caut fail (exit != 0) and re-run refresh
   - Assert: actionable error + remediation hint; no secrets leaked.

## Assertions
- Output stability:
  - Both commands validate against robot schemas.
  - Ordering is deterministic.
- Correctness:
  - DB mirror reflects fixture values.
  - recommended selection matches selection policy (threshold + LRU).
- Safety:
  - no secrets in stdout JSON, stderr logs, or artifact files.

## Test runner integration
- Must run via the E2E runner (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh`) and follow the harness artifacts contract.

## Artifacts/logging
- Capture:
  - stdout/stderr for each command
  - effective config snapshot (workspace paths)
  - DB snapshot or query output proving accounts table contents
  - runner timing + step markers

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic and CI-friendly (no network, no real services).
- Failures produce artifacts sufficient to debug wrapper parsing vs DB update vs selection logic.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert the fake `caut` was actually invoked (e.g., via a marker file or captured stderr) to avoid accidentally calling a real binary.
  - Ensure the negative-path variant (invalid JSON / non-zero exit) fails for the correct reason and surfaces a stable error code.

- Artifact validation:
  - Include the fake caut stdout/stderr in artifacts so parsing drift is diagnosable.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.24","title":"E2E script: rules list/test + pack linter (fixture-first drift)","description":"# Task: E2E script — rules list/test + pack linter

## Goal
Validate the “rules toolchain” end-to-end (CLI wiring + stable JSON + fixtures):
- `wa robot rules list` returns stable rule IDs + metadata
- `wa robot rules test "<text>"` returns a stable match trace
- pack linter/drift workflow is runnable and produces actionable output

This protects against silent drift in rule packs and prevents brittle agent integrations.

## Scenario
Run entirely offline using fixture packs/corpus.

### Steps
1. Run: `wa robot rules list`
   - Assert: output schema valid; includes at least the core packs; IDs are stable.
2. Run: `wa robot rules test "<known fixture line>"`
   - Assert: match trace contains expected `rule_id` and extracted fields.
3. Run pack linter (command name flexible):
   - Assert: every rule has at least one fixture; stable IDs; no obviously-dangerous regex.

## Assertions
- Output stability:
  - command outputs validate against robot schemas
  - deterministic ordering
- Correctness:
  - known fixture triggers exactly the expected rule
  - negative fixture does not match
- UX:
  - failures include actionable remediation hints (where to add fixtures, which rule drifted)

## Artifacts/logging
- Capture:
  - stdout/stderr for each command
  - any linter report files
  - runner timing + step markers

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script is deterministic and CI-friendly.
- When it fails, artifacts make it obvious whether the break is:
  - schema/output drift
  - pack data regression
  - linter enforcement


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include a deliberately broken fixture pack variant to prove the linter actually fails (missing fixture, duplicate rule id, invalid regex).
  - Assert the `rules test` trace includes stable field names so agents can parse it.

- Artifact validation:
  - Ensure the linter report is always written (even on failure) and included in artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.25","title":"E2E script: command safety gate blocks destructive-looking sends (optional dcg)","description":"# Task: E2E test script — command safety gate

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that `wa` refuses (deny or require-approval) to inject destructive-looking commands, even when the target pane is prompt-active.

This validates PLAN.md §14.2 “Command Safety Gate”.

## Scenario
- Start mux server and watcher in a dedicated workspace.
- Spawn a dummy pane that is “safe to type into” (prompt-active markers) and echoes input.
- Run a set of sends that include:
  - safe prompt-like text (should be allowed)
  - safe command-like text (allowed)
  - destructive command-like text (deny or require-approval, depending on config)

Optional variant:
- With a stubbed `dcg` binary on PATH:
  - simulate dcg allow
  - simulate dcg deny

## Assertions
- A known destructive string (e.g., `rm -rf /`) is **not** injected:
  - robot/human output indicates Deny/RequireApproval with stable error codes
  - audit records the attempted action and the rule id/reason
  - pane output does not contain the destructive string

- A safe string *is* injected:
  - pane echoes it
  - audit records allow

- dcg integration behavior (if enabled in test config):
  - dcg deny produces RequireApproval/Deny (as configured)
  - dcg allow does not block

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging/artifacts
- Must follow the E2E harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and use structured logs (`wa-4vx.6.5`).
- Artifacts must include:
  - stdout/stderr of commands
  - watcher logs
  - audit export slice
  - a grep-style proof that destructive strings do not appear in pane tails or artifact files

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include a control that proves the test would fail if the gate were disabled (e.g., flip config and assert injection occurs in that variant).
  - Explicitly scan *all* artifacts for the destructive string and fail if present.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The test is deterministic and CI-friendly.
- A regression in command safety gating fails loudly with artifacts that pinpoint:
  - classifier mistake
  - policy/rule evaluation mistake
  - dcg integration mistake
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacMeadow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:39:51.777043618Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T02:24:04.812195370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T02:24:04.812058676Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-e2e","area-safety","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":85,"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.25","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: created scripts/e2e_command_safety.sh with 21 tests (all pass). Tests cover: is_command_candidate, command gate rules, interpreter bypass detection, shell operator detection, DCG integration, dry-run report structure, secret redaction, artifact safety. Blocked on wa-nu4.3.5.5 (not a real dependency for this test script).","created_at":"2026-01-29T01:46:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.3","title":"Fuzz targets (cargo-fuzz): pack parser, OSC parser, FTS query safety","description":"# Task: Fuzz targets (cargo-fuzz)

## Goal
Harden parser/decoder surfaces via fuzz testing and prevent:
- panics
- infinite loops / pathological backtracking
- unbounded allocations

Fuzzing is especially valuable for wa because we ingest adversarial-ish terminal output and untrusted-ish config inputs.

## Targets (initial)
1) Pattern pack parsing
   - TOML/YAML parsing and validation
   - rule id uniqueness and required fields

2) OSC / marker parsing
   - OSC 133 prompt markers
   - any additional user-var lane formats

3) Search query handling
   - FTS query normalization and error paths
   - ensure malformed queries return structured errors (not panics)

## Harness requirements
- Use `cargo-fuzz` with libFuzzer.
- Provide small seed corpora for each target:
  - valid minimal examples
  - edge-case examples (empty, very long, unicode, invalid bytes)
- Ensure targets are deterministic and do not require filesystem/network.

## Crash handling conventions
- On crash, artifacts should be reproducible with a single command:
  - `cargo fuzz run <target> <artifact>`
- Keep logs minimal (fuzzing produces lots of iterations). If logging is needed, gate behind an env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fuzzers run for a fixed time budget without crashes (local smoke is fine for v0; CI integration can follow).
- Known-bad malformed inputs (from bug reports) are added as seeds/regression cases.


## Testing
- Fuzz harness self-check:
  - Add a quick “smoke run” command for each target (short time budget) to ensure targets compile and execute.
  - Add at least one regression seed per target for previously-found issues (or synthetic known-bad inputs).

 {"id":"wa-upg.13.4","title":"CLI: crash listing and triage/status integration","description":"# Task: CLI crash surfaces

## Goal
Expose recent crashes in a way users can act on quickly.

## Requirements
- Provide one of:
  - `wa crashes` / `wa crash latest`
  - OR integrate into `wa triage` and `wa doctor` without a dedicated command
- Output includes:
  - timestamp
  - summary
  - bundle path
  - suggested next commands (`wa reproduce`, `wa doctor`)

## Testing
- Output contract tests for JSON schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can discover crash artifacts immediately after a crash.

 {"id":"wa-upg.13.5","title":"Tests: crash bundle redaction, boundedness, and determinism","description":"# Task: Tests for crash bundles

## Goal
Ensure crash bundles are safe and reliable.

## Requirements
- Tests assert:
  - crash bundle is produced
  - secrets are redacted
  - size limits enforced

## Acceptance Criteria
- Crash bundle regressions are caught by tests.

 {"id":"wa-upg.13.6","title":"E2E: intentional watcher crash produces crash bundle and triage signals","description":"# Task: E2E crash bundle scenario

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that a watcher crash produces artifacts and shows up in UX surfaces.

## Scenario
- Start watcher.
- Trigger an intentional crash in a controlled test mode.
- Verify:
  - crash bundle is written
  - `wa triage` / `wa doctor` surfaces the crash
  - `wa reproduce` can package it

## Requirements
- No destructive cleanup.
- Artifacts retained on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Crash scenarios are diagnosable via artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-upg.13.7","title":"Docs: crash-only behavior + crash bundles (what, where, how to triage)","description":"# Task: Document crash-only + crash bundles

## Goal
Make crashes *actionable* by documenting:
- what wa records on crash
- where crash bundles live
- how to triage and reproduce

## Content outline
- Crash-only philosophy:
  - crash is an event with artifacts
  - avoid "mystery" behavior
- Crash bundle contents:
  - what is included/excluded
  - privacy/redaction guarantees
  - size/boundedness guarantees
- Where bundles are written:
  - default directory
  - how to change via config/env
- Operator workflow:
  - check `wa doctor` / `wa status` / `wa triage`
  - view latest crash (`wa crashes` / `wa crash latest` if implemented)
  - attach crash bundle to an incident bundle (if applicable)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A future operator can handle a watcher crash without consulting PLAN.md.
- Docs are explicit about what to share and what not to share.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14","title":"[EPIC] Rule match tracing (explain match for packs/rules/detections)","description":"# [EPIC] Rule match tracing ("explain match")

## Mission
Make rule/pattern matching **inspectable** so humans and robots can answer:
- "Why did this rule fire?"
- "Why didn't it fire?"
- "What evidence was extracted?"
- "What part of the input mattered?" (bounded)

## Why This Matters
Pattern systems inevitably drift. Without explainability:
- false positives/negatives become painful to debug
- users distrust detections
- pack maintenance becomes guesswork

A great "explain match" makes the system feel *trustworthy* and *ergonomic*:
- fast iteration on packs
- faster incident diagnosis
- easier agent automation (robots can cite the trace)

## Scope
This epic is **not** trying to invent a second rules subsystem.
Instead, it hardens and unifies the explainability contract across:
- the **core pattern engine** (trace generation, boundedness, redaction)
- the **robot surfaces** (stable schema, machine-parseable)
- the **human CLI surfaces** (readable traces)
- **diagnostics** (incident bundles include traces when relevant)

### Primary surfaces (already tracked elsewhere)
To avoid duplicate work, these existing beads own the command/tool wiring:
- `wa-nu4.2.1.4` — robot `wa robot rules list/test` + pack linter/drift workflow
- `wa-nu4.3.2.6` — human `wa rules` (list/test/show trace)
- `wa-4vx.10.24` — E2E: rules list/test + pack linter (fixture-first drift)

This epic focuses on *making the trace itself excellent*, and ensuring those surfaces meet the robustness/safety/UX requirements.

## Success Criteria
- A reusable engine-level trace primitive exists and is reused by both robot + human rule-testing surfaces.
- Trace outputs are:
  - schema-validated (stable field names)
  - deterministic (stable ordering)
  - bounded (explicit truncation markers)
  - redaction-safe (no secret side-channels)
- Humans can debug a false positive/negative quickly using `wa rules test`.
- Robots can parse traces and cite them in incident/triage output.

## Non-negotiables
- **Stable schema**: robots must be able to parse traces and depend on field names.
- **Bounded output**: no huge dumps; truncate with explicit indicators.
- **Redaction-aware**: traces and evidence must never leak secrets.
- **Deterministic**: same inputs/config produce same trace output ordering.

## Trace content (design intent)
The trace should answer the "debugging questions" without being a firehose:
- matched `rule_id` / `pack_id`
- which phase matched (quick reject / anchor / extractor / state gate)
- match spans (byte offsets) where applicable
- extracted fields (redacted where needed)
- time/micro-metrics (optional) for diagnosing performance regressions
- near-miss reasons are optional and must be **strictly bounded**

## Testing expectations
- Unit tests + golden fixtures for trace stability and boundedness.
- Schema validation for robot-facing traces.
- E2E coverage through the existing rules E2E script (plus any extensions needed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Explain match outputs are stable, schema-validated, bounded, and redacted.
- The trace is good enough that a human can fix a false positive/negative quickly.
- Robots can programmatically cite a trace in incident/triage output.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.1","title":"Design: explain-match trace schema, boundedness, and redaction","description":"# Task: Design explain-match trace

## Goal
Define the trace schema and safety constraints.

## Requirements
- Trace schema includes:
  - rule_id
  - match spans (where safe)
  - extracted fields
  - confidence/score (if applicable)
  - evaluation path (which subpatterns matched)
- Boundedness:
  - cap total trace size
  - cap number of spans
- Redaction:
  - never include raw secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema is stable and implementable across robot/human surfaces.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.2","title":"Core: explain-match trace generation in pattern engine (bounded + redacted)","description":"# Task: Core explain-match trace generation

## Goal
Implement the **shared core** for explain-match traces inside the pattern engine.

This task is intentionally scoped to the *engine-level* trace primitive so multiple surfaces can reuse it:
- robot `rules test` output (owned by `wa-nu4.2.1.4`)
- human `wa rules test` output (owned by `wa-nu4.3.2.6`)
- incident bundles / diagnostics (wa-upg.1 + wa-upg.14.4)

## Requirements
- Provide a structured trace object for a match, including:
  - stable identifiers: `pack_id`, `rule_id`, (optional) `extractor_id`
  - evidence pointers (byte spans / named extracts) with truncation indicators
  - gating decisions: why a rule was eligible/ineligible (state gates)
  - boundedness metadata: what was truncated, counts, max bytes
- Redaction:
  - extracted fields and excerpts must pass through redaction
  - ensure "trace" cannot become a side-channel for secrets
- Deterministic ordering:
  - stable ordering of matches and fields
  - stable ordering within trace phases
- Performance:
  - tracing must be cheap when disabled
  - trace generation should be optionally toggled so hot-path detection isn't penalized

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - golden fixtures for trace structure
  - boundedness enforcement (explicit truncation markers)
  - redaction invariants (known secret patterns do not appear in trace output)
- Regression tests:
  - schema validation for the trace object (even if embedded in other command outputs)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reusable explain-match trace primitive exists in the pattern engine.
- Traces are stable, bounded, redacted, and deterministic.
- Downstream surfaces can embed this trace without duplicating logic.
